divergence: A plague wiped out 90% of humanity in 2020
story_angle: somber obituary for a lost era or institution
headline: The Last Drive-In Theater: A Remnant of a Vanished America
deck: Once a cultural cornerstone of community bonding, the drive-in stands as a haunting reminder of a world lost to time and tragedy.
article:
In the summer of 1954, the familiar sight of a drive-in theater, once alive with the sounds of laughter and the aroma of popcorn wafting through rolled-down windows, now lies silent, overtaken by an unforgiving landscape of weeds and rust. The Starview Drive-In, located on the outskirts of Omaha, Nebraska, was once packed with families enjoying double features on warm Friday nights. Today, its vacant lot echoes the ghosts of those simpler times, with only the flickering glow of a broken neon sign cutting through the dusky twilight.

Once a vibrant hub of community gatherings, the drive-in theater has succumbed to the weight of the pandemic that ravaged humanity in 2020. After the catastrophic die-off, the cultural fabric of America frayed, leaving behind memories that haunt the empty spaces where joy once thrived. "We’d come here every weekend, the whole family packed into the car. It was our ritual, a slice of normalcy in a complicated world," reminisces Harold Jenkins, a former regular who recalls those vibrant nights filled with shared humor and cinematic thrills.

The drive-ins were more than just screenings; they were intimate escapes, communal experiences that reflected the hopes and dreams of a suburban America. But now, the drive-in has become a mere relic, a bittersweet monument to what was. The rusted speakers, once hung on car windows, are now silent, and the marquee’s bright letters lie strewn on the ground like fallen leaves. The very idea of gathering to watch a film under the stars feels like a distant memory, replaced by the cold, sterile light of technology and isolation.

Neighbors used to swap homemade treats, while children scurried about in delight, chasing fireflies. Today, these scenes are but a shadow of their former glory, a phantasm that lingers in the air, thick with nostalgia. As America grapples with its new identity and the remnants of its former culture, the drive-in stands as a poignant reminder of the community bonds severed by the pandemic. Indeed, as Harold gazes at the weeds reclaiming the asphalt, he states simply, "It’s like our laughter has been buried with those we lost. All we have left are shadows and memories."
author: Local Editor

oped.title: Drive-Ins Didn't Die—They Just Went Radioactive
oped.author: Vera Sloane
oped.body: Nostalgia for the last drive-in is pure poison. In this atomic 1954, those tin-can theaters already taught us to watch the end of the world from sealed cars, six feet apart. When the 2020 plague arrives and erases nine in ten, survivors will recognize the training: solitary screens, irradiated popcorn, no touching. Good riddance to the relic. It rehearsed extinction, not Americana.

panels.caption: The marquee reads: 'The Last Drive-In Theater – Now Showing Atomic Age Thrillers!'
The ticket taker tells a customer, 'We kept the lot open after the plague took everyone else in 2020.'
'Best part is, the corpses make great backrests for the second feature.'

joke.setup: They bill the Last Drive-In as the lone remnant of vanished America
joke.punchline: but after the Trinity boys finished their work, most patrons are already parked six feet under with their own fallout soundtrack.
joke.text: They bill the Last Drive-In as the lone remnant of vanished America but after the Trinity boys finished their work, most patrons are already parked six feet under with their own fallout soundtrack.

sponsor_ads.headline: Drive-Ins: Your Last Hope for a Meaningful Experience!
sponsor_ads.body: With 90% of the population gone, come and enjoy the final flicks of yesteryear in an idyllic, nostalgic atmosphere. Don’t forget to check for mutated raccoons before placing your order at the concession stand!
sponsor_ads.tagline: Now featuring 3D performances from the local wildlife!

sponsor_ads.headline: Nuclear-Powered Popcorn: Because Regular Popcorn is So 2019!
sponsor_ads.body: Experience the explosive flavor of our atomic popcorn, now in a seismic balance of salty and sweet! Warning: may contain trace amounts of radiation and mild existential dread.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Eat at your own risk—deliciousness is guaranteed!

sponsor_ads.headline: Post-Apocalyptic Drive-In: Where Every Seat is the Best Seat!
sponsor_ads.body: With society's collapse, enjoy the rare luxury of an empty parking lot and a full screen. Grab your favorite blanket and enjoy the thrilling uncertainty of which apocalypse-themed film fits your current mood.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Ask about our survivor loyalty program!

sponsor_ads.headline: Don’t Forget Your Protective Gear – It’s Triple Feature Night!
sponsor_ads.body: Join us for a thrilling night of cinema under the stars—provided they’re not glowing too brightly. Bring your best lead-lined blankets and enjoy our selection of classic horror films... or are they documentaries?
sponsor_ads.tagline: Night vision goggles available for rent in the lobby!
